content.text: Billing Accounts

An Azure billing account is the top-level entity in the Azure billing hierarchy. It represents the agreement between you (or your organisation) and Microsoft that governs how you pay for Azure services. The type of billing account you have determines what management capabilities are available, how invoices are structured, and what roles exist for cost management.

There are four billing account types, each corresponding to a different agreement model: MOSP (Microsoft Online Subscription Program, also called Pay-As-You-Go), MCA (Microsoft Customer Agreement), MPA (Microsoft Partner Agreement), and EA (Enterprise Agreement). Most new direct Azure customers use MCA. Large enterprises typically use EA.

attrs.alt: Azure billing account types and their hierarchy structures

content.text: Billing Account Types Compared
Type
Who Uses It
Structure
Payment Method
Volume Discounts
MOSP (PAYG)
Individuals, small businesses signing up directly
1 billing account = 1 subscription
Credit card or bank transfer
None
MCA (Microsoft Customer Agreement)
Small to mid-size organisations, modern enterprise
Billing Account > Billing Profile > Invoice Section > Subscription
Credit card, wire transfer, or PO
Through negotiated pricing or commitment
MPA (Microsoft Partner Agreement)
CSP (Cloud Solution Provider) partners
Billing Account > Billing Profile > Customer > Subscription
Partner billed by Microsoft; customers billed by partner
Partner margin and customer discounts
EA (Enterprise Agreement)
Large enterprises (500+ seats, $1M+ annual Azure spend)
Enrollment > Department > Account > Subscription
Upfront monetary commitment, annual invoice
Significant volume discounts, prepaid commit

As of 2024, Microsoft is transitioning EA customers to MCA-E (Microsoft Customer Agreement for Enterprise) at renewal time. The MCA-E offers similar commercial terms as EA but with the modern MCA hierarchy and programmatic management capabilities.
Viewing Your Billing Account

# List billing accounts you have access to
az billing account list \
  --query "[].{Name:name, DisplayName:displayName, AgreementType:agreementType, Status:accountStatus}" \
  --output table

# Get details of a specific billing account
az billing account show \
  --name <billing-account-name> \
  --output json

# List billing profiles within an MCA billing account
az billing profile list \
  --account-name <billing-account-name> \
  --query "[].{Name:name, DisplayName:displayName, Status:status, Currency:currency}" \
  --output table

from azure.identity import DefaultAzureCredential
from azure.mgmt.billing import BillingManagementClient

credential = DefaultAzureCredential()
billing = BillingManagementClient(credential)

# List all billing accounts accessible to the authenticated identity
for account in billing.billing_accounts.list():
    print(f"Billing Account: {account.display_name}")
    print(f"  ID:             {account.name}")
    print(f"  Agreement:      {account.agreement_type}")
    print(f"  Status:         {account.account_status}")

    # For MCA accounts, list billing profiles
    if account.agreement_type == "MicrosoftCustomerAgreement":
        for profile in billing.billing_profiles.list_by_billing_account(account.name):
            print(f"  Profile: {profile.display_name} ({profile.status})")
MCA Hierarchy: Billing Profiles and Invoice Sections
Under an MCA billing account, the hierarchy has two levels below the billing account before you reach subscriptions:

Billing Profile: corresponds to a separate invoice and payment method. An organisation typically creates one billing profile per legal entity, currency, or payment method. Each billing profile has its own invoice PDF generated monthly.

Invoice Section: a cost-allocation grouping within a billing profile. Used to separate costs by department, team, project, or cost centre. Subscriptions are assigned to an invoice section, and the invoice section appears as a line item grouping on the billing profile's invoice.

# List invoice sections within a billing profile
az billing invoice-section list \
  --account-name <billing-account-name> \
  --profile-name <billing-profile-name> \
  --query "[].{Name:name, DisplayName:displayName, State:state}" \
  --output table

# Create a new invoice section (for a new team or department)
az billing invoice-section create \
  --account-name <billing-account-name> \
  --profile-name <billing-profile-name> \
  --invoice-section-name "platform-team" \
  --display-name "Platform Engineering Team"

# Move a subscription to a different invoice section
az billing subscription update \
  --account-name <billing-account-name> \
  --subscription-id <sub-id> \
  --invoice-section-name "platform-team" \
  --billing-profile-name <profile-name>
EA Hierarchy: Enrollments, Departments, and Accounts
EA customers manage their billing through the EA portal (ea.azure.com) or the Azure Portal under Cost Management + Billing. The hierarchy maps to organisational structure:
Enrollment: the top-level EA contract, identified by an enrollment number. It has a monetary commitment (prepaid amount) and a term (typically 3 years).
Department: an optional grouping level representing a business unit or department. Departments have their own spending quotas and administrators.
Account: maps to an individual subscription owner's email address. An account owner can create subscriptions under the account.
Subscription: the Azure subscription where resources are deployed.

# EA management requires EA portal access or the Azure Billing REST API
# CLI commands for EA use the billing account scope

# List EA departments
az billing department list \
  --account-name <enrollment-number> \
  --output table

# List EA enrollment accounts (subscription owner accounts)
az billing enrollment-account list \
  --output table

# Create a subscription under an EA enrollment account
az account create \
  --enrollment-account-name <enrollment-account-object-id> \
  --offer-type MS-AZR-0017P \
  --display-name "production-app-team-01"

# Get EA balance (monetary commitment remaining)
az billing account show \
  --name <enrollment-number> \
  --expand "billingProfiles/invoices" \
  --query "enrollmentDetails.prepaidBalance" \
  --output json
Billing Roles and Access Control
Billing accounts have their own role model separate from Azure RBAC. Billing roles control who can view and manage billing information — they do not grant rights to deploy Azure resources.
Scope
Role
Permissions
Billing Account
Billing Account Owner
Full billing account management; manage all profiles, sections, subscriptions
Billing Account
Billing Account Contributor
Manage billing but cannot assign billing roles to others
Billing Account
Billing Account Reader
Read-only view of billing account, profiles, invoices
Billing Profile
Billing Profile Owner
Manage profile: payment methods, invoices, subscriptions
Billing Profile
Billing Profile Contributor
Manage profile but cannot assign roles
Billing Profile
Invoice Manager
Download invoices for the profile; cannot modify anything
Invoice Section
Invoice Section Owner
Manage subscriptions within the section; create new subscriptions
Invoice Section
Invoice Section Contributor
Manage subscriptions in section; cannot assign roles
Invoice Section
Azure Subscription Creator
Create subscriptions in the section; no other billing access

# List billing role assignments on a billing account
az billing role-assignment list \
  --account-name <billing-account-name> \
  --output table

# Assign Billing Account Reader to a user
az billing role-assignment create \
  --account-name <billing-account-name> \
  --role-definition-name "Billing Account Reader" \
  --principal-id <user-object-id>

# Assign Invoice Section Owner for a specific team
az billing role-assignment create \
  --account-name <billing-account-name> \
  --billing-profile-name <profile-name> \
  --invoice-section-name <section-name> \
  --role-definition-name "Invoice Section Owner" \
  --principal-id <user-object-id>
Downloading and Parsing Invoices

Invoices are generated at the billing profile level (MCA) or enrollment level (EA) and are available as PDF and usage CSV files. Programmatic access lets you automate invoice archival or cost reporting.

from azure.identity import DefaultAzureCredential
from azure.mgmt.billing import BillingManagementClient
import requests, os

credential = DefaultAzureCredential()
billing = BillingManagementClient(credential)

account_name = "<billing-account-name>"
profile_name = "<billing-profile-name>"

# List recent invoices for a billing profile
for invoice in billing.invoices.list_by_billing_profile(account_name, profile_name):
    print(f"Invoice: {invoice.name}")
    print(f"  Period:  {invoice.invoice_period_start_date} to {invoice.invoice_period_end_date}")
    print(f"  Amount:  {invoice.amount_due.value} {invoice.amount_due.currency}")
    print(f"  Status:  {invoice.status}")

    # Download the PDF
    if invoice.documents:
        for doc in invoice.documents:
            if doc.kind == "Invoice":
                pdf_url = doc.url
                token = credential.get_token("https://management.azure.com/.default").token
                resp = requests.get(pdf_url, headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"})
                fname = f"invoice-{invoice.name}.pdf"
                with open(fname, "wb") as f:
                    f.write(resp.content)
                print(f"  Saved: {fname}")
Creating Subscriptions Programmatically

In large organisations, creating subscriptions manually through the Portal does not scale. The Azure Billing API allows you to create subscriptions under EA enrollment accounts or MCA invoice sections programmatically, which enables subscription vending — automatically provisioning a new subscription when a team requests one.

from azure.identity import DefaultAzureCredential
from azure.mgmt.subscription import SubscriptionClient
from azure.mgmt.subscription.models import (
    ModernSubscriptionCreationParameters,
    AdditionalParameters,
)

credential = DefaultAzureCredential()
sub_client = SubscriptionClient(credential)

# Create a subscription under an MCA invoice section
creation_params = ModernSubscriptionCreationParameters(
    display_name="team-alpha-production",
    sku_id="0001",  # Microsoft Azure Plan
    billing_profile_name="<billing-profile-name>",
    invoice_section_name="<invoice-section-name>",
    additional_parameters=AdditionalParameters(
        management_group_id="/providers/Microsoft.Management/managementGroups/<mg-id>",
    ),
)

# This is an async long-running operation
poller = sub_client.subscription_factory.begin_create_subscription(
    billing_account_name="<billing-account-name>",
    billing_profile_name="<billing-profile-name>",
    invoice_section_name="<invoice-section-name>",
    body=creation_params,
)
result = poller.result()
print(f"Created subscription: {result.subscription_id}")
print(f"Display name: {result.display_name}")

# Create a subscription under an EA enrollment account (CLI)
az account create \
  --enrollment-account-name <enrollment-account-object-id> \
  --offer-type MS-AZR-0017P \
  --display-name "team-beta-production"

# Create a subscription and immediately move it to a management group
NEW_SUB_ID=$(az account create \
  --enrollment-account-name <enrollment-account-object-id> \
  --offer-type MS-AZR-0017P \
  --display-name "app-team-01" \
  --query "subscriptionId" --output tsv)

az management-group subscription add \
  --name <management-group-name> \
  --subscription $NEW_SUB_ID

echo "Created and enrolled subscription: $NEW_SUB_ID"
Subscription Vending with Azure Landing Zones

Subscription vending is a pattern where a central platform team provides a self-service mechanism for application teams to request new Azure subscriptions. The platform team defines the guardrails (management group placement, policy assignment, networking baseline) and automates the provisioning.

# Minimal subscription vending workflow
# 1. Receive request (team name, environment, cost centre)
# 2. Create subscription under appropriate invoice section
# 3. Move to correct management group
# 4. Apply required tags
# 5. Assign RBAC roles to the requesting team

import json, requests
from azure.identity import DefaultAzureCredential
from azure.mgmt.resource import ResourceManagementClient
from azure.mgmt.authorization import AuthorizationManagementClient
from azure.mgmt.authorization.models import RoleAssignmentCreateParameters

credential = DefaultAzureCredential()
token = credential.get_token("https://management.azure.com/.default").token

def tag_subscription(sub_id: str, tags: dict):
    url = f"https://management.azure.com/subscriptions/{sub_id}/providers/Microsoft.Resources/tags/default?api-version=2021-04-01"
    body = {"properties": {"tags": tags}}
    resp = requests.put(url, json=body, headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"})
    resp.raise_for_status()
    print(f"Tagged subscription {sub_id}: {tags}")

def assign_role(sub_id: str, principal_id: str, role_definition_id: str):
    import uuid
    client = AuthorizationManagementClient(credential, sub_id)
    assignment_name = str(uuid.uuid4())
    client.role_assignments.create(
        scope=f"/subscriptions/{sub_id}",
        role_assignment_name=assignment_name,
        parameters=RoleAssignmentCreateParameters(
            role_definition_id=role_definition_id,
            principal_id=principal_id,
            principal_type="Group",
        ),
    )
    print(f"Assigned role {role_definition_id} to {principal_id} on /subscriptions/{sub_id}")

# Example usage after creating a subscription
sub_id = "<newly-created-subscription-id>"
team_group_id = "<aad-group-object-id>"

tag_subscription(sub_id, {
    "team": "platform-alpha",
    "environment": "production",
    "cost-center": "ENG-42",
    "owner": "team-alpha@example.com",
})

# Assign Contributor role to the team's AAD group
CONTRIBUTOR = "/providers/Microsoft.Authorization/roleDefinitions/b24988ac-6180-42a0-ab88-20f7382dd24c"
assign_role(sub_id, team_group_id, CONTRIBUTOR)
Monitoring Billing Account Health

Billing accounts can have alerts when spend exceeds thresholds, payment methods expire, or invoices become overdue. Programmatic monitoring ensures finance teams are notified without relying on manual portal checks.

from azure.identity import DefaultAzureCredential
from azure.mgmt.billing import BillingManagementClient
from azure.mgmt.consumption import ConsumptionManagementClient
import datetime

credential = DefaultAzureCredential()
billing = BillingManagementClient(credential)
account_name = "<billing-account-name>"
profile_name = "<billing-profile-name>"

# Check payment method status
payment_methods = list(billing.payment_methods.list_by_billing_profile(
    account_name, profile_name
))
for pm in payment_methods:
    print(f"Payment method: {pm.display_name} ({pm.payment_method_type})")
    if hasattr(pm, "expiration") and pm.expiration:
        exp = pm.expiration
        days_remaining = (exp - datetime.date.today()).days
        if days_remaining < 60:
            print(f"  WARNING: expires in {days_remaining} days!")

# List overdue invoices
for invoice in billing.invoices.list_by_billing_profile(account_name, profile_name):
    if invoice.status == "PastDue":
        print(f"OVERDUE invoice: {invoice.name} - {invoice.amount_due.value} {invoice.amount_due.currency}")
        print(f"  Due date: {invoice.due_date}")
Transferring Subscriptions Between Billing Accounts

You may need to move a subscription from one billing account to another — for example, when a customer migrates from MOSP to MCA, or when a team moves to a different department's billing profile. Azure supports subscription transfers, though the process differs by account type.
The destination billing account must be of a compatible type (e.g. MOSP to MCA is supported; EA to MCA requires a full transfer process).
Initiate the transfer from the destination billing account — the source account owner receives an email with a transfer link.
The source subscription owner accepts the transfer request.
The subscription is moved; existing resources continue running without interruption.
Historical cost data does not transfer — it remains in the source billing account.

# Initiate a transfer request (from destination billing account)
# The recipient email must be the owner of the source subscription
az billing transfer initiate \
  --account-name <destination-billing-account> \
  --billing-profile-name <destination-profile> \
  --invoice-section-name <destination-section> \
  --recipient-email-id "subscription-owner@example.com"

# List pending transfer requests
az billing transfer list \
  --account-name <billing-account-name> \
  --billing-profile-name <billing-profile-name> \
  --invoice-section-name <invoice-section-name> \
  --output table

# Cancel a pending transfer
az billing transfer cancel \
  --account-name <billing-account-name> \
  --billing-profile-name <billing-profile-name> \
  --invoice-section-name <invoice-section-name> \
  --transfer-name <transfer-name>

Subscription transfers do not interrupt running workloads. However, any Azure Marketplace purchases, Reserved Instances, or Savings Plans associated with the subscription need to be handled separately — they do not transfer automatically.
Cost Export Configuration per Billing Profile

Azure Cost Management exports deliver scheduled CSV/Parquet files to Blob Storage. Configuring exports at the billing profile scope ensures every subscription in that profile is covered without per-subscription setup.
Listing and Creating Exports via REST API

import requests
from azure.identity import DefaultAzureCredential

credential = DefaultAzureCredential()
token = credential.get_token("https://management.azure.com/.default").token
headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}", "Content-Type": "application/json"}

billing_account_name = "1234567"          # enrollment number or GUID
billing_profile_name = "ABCD-1234-EF"    # billing profile ID

# List existing exports
list_url = (
    f"https://management.azure.com/providers/Microsoft.Billing/"
    f"billingAccounts/{billing_account_name}/"
    f"billingProfiles/{billing_profile_name}/"
    f"providers/Microsoft.CostManagement/exports"
    f"?api-version=2023-11-01"
)
resp = requests.get(list_url, headers=headers)
exports = resp.json().get("value", [])
for e in exports:
    print(e["name"], e["properties"]["schedule"]["status"])
Triggering an On-Demand Export Run

az costmanagement export trigger \
  --billing-account-id 1234567 \
  --billing-profile-id ABCD-1234-EF \
  --name monthly-actual-cost
Billing Alerts and Budget Management
Budget alerts notify stakeholders when spend approaches or exceeds defined thresholds. Budgets can be scoped at the billing account, billing profile, or individual subscription level.
Creating a Billing Account Budget with Alert Contacts
Listing Budget Status and Remaining Balance

from azure.mgmt.consumption import ConsumptionManagementClient
from azure.identity import DefaultAzureCredential

client = ConsumptionManagementClient(DefaultAzureCredential(), "<subscription-id>")

# List budgets at subscription scope
for budget in client.budgets.list("/subscriptions/<subscription-id>"):
    spent = budget.current_spend.amount if budget.current_spend else 0
    remaining = budget.amount - spent
    pct = (spent / budget.amount * 100) if budget.amount else 0
    print(
        f"{budget.name}: limit={budget.amount:.0f} "
        f"spent={spent:.0f} ({pct:.1f}%) remaining={remaining:.0f}"
    )
Enterprise Agreement Balance and Credit Monitoring
EA customers prepay Azure credits (formerly 'Monetary Commitment'). Monitoring the drawdown rate helps avoid unexpected PAYG charges when credits are exhausted.

import requests
from azure.identity import DefaultAzureCredential

credential = DefaultAzureCredential()
token = credential.get_token("https://management.azure.com/.default").token
headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}

billing_account_name = "1234567"  # EA enrollment number

# Get balance summary for current billing period
url = (
    f"https://management.azure.com/providers/Microsoft.Billing/"
    f"billingAccounts/{billing_account_name}/"
    f"billingPeriods/202401/"
    f"providers/Microsoft.Consumption/balances"
    f"?api-version=2019-10-01"
)
resp = requests.get(url, headers=headers)
balance = resp.json().get("properties", {})

print(f"Beginning Balance:   {balance.get('beginningBalance', 0):>12,.2f}")
print(f"New Purchases:       {balance.get('newPurchases', 0):>12,.2f}")
print(f"Adjustments:         {balance.get('adjustments', 0):>12,.2f}")
print(f"Utilized:            {balance.get('utilized', 0):>12,.2f}")
print(f"Service Overage:     {balance.get('serviceOverage', 0):>12,.2f}")
print(f"Charges Billed Sep:  {balance.get('chargesBilledSeparately', 0):>12,.2f}")
print(f"Total Overage:       {balance.get('totalOverage', 0):>12,.2f}")
print(f"Ending Balance:      {balance.get('endingBalance', 0):>12,.2f}")

# Burn rate — project when credits will be exhausted
def project_credit_exhaustion(billing_account_name: str, lookback_months: int = 3) -> dict:
    """Estimate remaining months of EA credit based on recent burn rate."""
    import requests
    from datetime import datetime, timedelta
    from azure.identity import DefaultAzureCredential

    credential = DefaultAzureCredential()
    token = credential.get_token("https://management.azure.com/.default").token
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}

    utilized_amounts = []
    base_url = (
        f"https://management.azure.com/providers/Microsoft.Billing/"
        f"billingAccounts/{billing_account_name}"
    )

    now = datetime.utcnow()
    for i in range(lookback_months, 0, -1):
        period = (now.replace(day=1) - timedelta(days=30 * (i - 1))).strftime("%Y%m")
        url = f"{base_url}/billingPeriods/{period}/providers/Microsoft.Consumption/balances?api-version=2019-10-01"
        resp = requests.get(url, headers=headers)
        if resp.ok:
            props = resp.json().get("properties", {})
            utilized_amounts.append(props.get("utilized", 0))

    if not utilized_amounts:
        return {"error": "no data"}

    avg_monthly_burn = sum(utilized_amounts) / len(utilized_amounts)

    # Get current ending balance
    current_period = now.strftime("%Y%m")
    url = f"{base_url}/billingPeriods/{current_period}/providers/Microsoft.Consumption/balances?api-version=2019-10-01"
    resp = requests.get(url, headers=headers)
    ending_balance = resp.json().get("properties", {}).get("endingBalance", 0)

    months_remaining = ending_balance / avg_monthly_burn if avg_monthly_burn > 0 else float("inf")
    exhaustion_date = now + timedelta(days=months_remaining * 30)

    return {
        "ending_balance": ending_balance,
        "avg_monthly_burn": avg_monthly_burn,
        "months_remaining": round(months_remaining, 1),
        "projected_exhaustion": exhaustion_date.strftime("%Y-%m-%d")
    }

result = project_credit_exhaustion("1234567", lookback_months=3)
print(result)
EA balance queries use billing-period scoped endpoints (/billingPeriods/{YYYYMM}/). The period string is six digits: four-digit year followed by two-digit month (e.g., 202401 for January 2024).
Summary

Azure billing accounts are the top-level billing entities that determine your agreement type (MOSP, MCA, MPA, or EA) and the hierarchy available for cost management. MCA is the modern standard with a three-level hierarchy (Billing Profile > Invoice Section > Subscription) suitable for teams and departments. EA enrollments suit large enterprises with volume commitment. Use billing roles — separate from Azure RBAC — to grant finance teams invoice access without giving them resource management rights, and use invoice sections to attribute costs to specific teams or projects without separate billing profiles.
